_note: ORIGINAL content. Think C++ and the Google C++ Class outline informed coverage only; no prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Pointers
summary: Understand addresses, pointers, and safe access to another value.
refresher: A pointer stores the address of another value. `&value` gets an address, `*pointer` follows it, and `nullptr` means the pointer currently points to nothing. Check for `nullptr` before dereferencing.

blocks.body: Every variable lives somewhere in memory. A pointer stores that location instead of storing another copy of the value. This lets code reach or change the original value through its address.

In `int* pointer = &score;`, the star says `pointer` stores the address of an integer, and `&score` gets score's address. Later, `*pointer` follows that address to read or change score. The star has two related jobs: declaring a pointer and following one.

blocks.title: Python usually hides this layer
blocks.body: Python variables also let code refer to objects, but Python manages those references for you. C++ exposes addresses directly when you use pointers, which gives more control and also creates more responsibility.

blocks.caption: The pointer stores score's address. Assigning through `*pointer` therefore changes score itself.
Check for a valid target before following an address.

blocks.body: The first pointer contains `nullptr`, which means there is no integer to follow. Dereferencing it is invalid and may crash the program. The condition in the second version makes sure a real address exists first.

blocks.title: Never follow an unknown address
blocks.body: Initialize a pointer when you create it. Use `nullptr` when it has no target, then check before using `*pointer`. Dereferencing a null or uninitialized pointer is invalid.

blocks.why: The `&` operator gets x's memory address, and the pointer stores that address.

blocks.prompt: What does `*p` mean in an expression?
choices: the value at the pointed address
blocks.why: Dereference follows the stored address and accesses the object located there.

blocks.title: Pointers checklist
items: Prefer references when null is not meaningful.
